title: Build the long-form teaching framework
objective: Give every lesson a reusable structure for goals, extended explanations, guided examples, practice, and further reading.
acceptanceCriteria: A typed guide schema supports multiple explanatory paragraphs, step-by-step walkthroughs, guided practice, and annotated external resources
The lesson page renders the new content accessibly and responsively
Further-reading links distinguish articles, courses, papers, and videos
details: Create lesson-guides.ts, rich lesson UI components, progressive-disclosure practice answers, and styling/tests.

title: Expand Foundations and Architecture
objective: Make lessons 1-12 understandable without assumed ML knowledge.
acceptanceCriteria: All 12 lessons have substantial concept-specific guides
Mathematical notation is explained in words and through worked reasoning
Each lesson has active practice and vetted further reading
details: Add beginner-first narrative, analogies, walkthroughs, examples, exercises, and resources for Foundations and Architecture.

title: Expand Pre-Training and Post-Training
objective: Turn lessons 13-28 into complete explanations of training and behavioral shaping.
acceptanceCriteria: All 16 lessons explain mechanisms, trade-offs, and failure modes in depth
Case studies explicitly connect prior components into decisions
Every guide includes practice and external resources
details: Add long-form guides for objectives, data, systems, evaluation, SFT, preferences, RL, safety, and both case studies.

title: Expand Inference, Applications, and Advanced
objective: Make lessons 29-44 practical and conceptually complete for a beginner.
acceptanceCriteria: All 16 lessons have substantial guides with system-level examples
Security, evaluation, and interpretability distinguish claims from controls
Every guide includes active practice and further reading
details: Add guides for decoding/serving, RAG/agents/reliability, compression, multimodality, and interpretability/editing.

title: Turn every capstone into a guided project
objective: Give students enough structure, workspace, feedback, and assessment to actually create and learn from each capstone.
acceptanceCriteria: All seven capstones state the scenario, learning goals, estimated effort, prerequisites, and concrete deliverables
Every project has staged instructions, editable learner workspace, checkpoints/hints, and persistent local draft state
Every project has a detailed rubric, model response, reflection prompts, and explicit completion criteria
details: Expand the synthesis briefs into guided projects with structured instructions, local draft persistence, self-assessment, exemplars, and responsive UI.

title: Audit depth, accuracy, and live usability
objective: Verify the expanded lessons and capstones are genuinely instructive rather than merely longer.
acceptanceCriteria: Automated tests prove all 44 lessons have complete guides and valid resource links
Long-form content and practice work on desktop and mobile without overflow
All seven capstone workspaces persist drafts and expose instructions, deliverables, rubric, exemplar, and reflection
A skeptical audit finds no sparse, generic, duplicated, or misleading lesson guides or project instructions
details: Run schema/content/project audits, lint/build/tests, live browser checks, and fix every detected gap.
